name: Boiled Frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine sugar, corn syrup, and boiling water in a large bowl.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil, stirring until the sugar is completely dissolved.
3. Boil the mixture rapidly without stirring until it forms a soft ball when dropped into cold water (240°F) or a thread forms when dropped from the tip of a spoon.
4. Pour the hot syrup over the beaten egg whites in a fine stream while continuously beating with a rotary egg beater.
5. Add vanilla extract and continue beating for 10 to 15 minutes, or until the frosting is cool and has reached the proper consistency for spreading.
6. If the frosting becomes too stiff to beat with an egg beater, switch to using a wooden spoon.
7. Make enough frosting to cover the tops and sides of two 9-inch layer cakes.
